changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "It Was Delicious"

TextENGNew: ``Hey, Mion. Here.``
``Wha— Wh-What is it, Kei-chan...?``
I thrust the bento box she’d given me yesterday right at her.
``It’s the bento box you gave me yesterday. Thanks for the meal.@k@vS20/01/440100066. It was delicious.``
...Mion’s face gradually flushed red.
changeReason: "gradually flushed red" more accurately captures ぱーーっと薄く (spreading lightly) compared to "quickly flushed bright red" which overstates both speed and intensity.

TextENGNew: ...Hey, hey. The bento was supposed to have come from Shion, remember...
What’s the point of turning red when you’re being Mion right now...
At this rate, she’d dig her own grave and self-destruct, so I decided to throw her a lifeline.

``Listen. I was starving yesterday, right?@k@vS20/01/440100068. Shion went out of her way to bring a bento all the way to my house.@k@vS20/01/440100069. This is that bento box.@k@vS20/01/440100070. I washed it properly, just so you know!``
``Ah... ahaha, r-right! Shion’s really thoughtful, huh~!``
That was painfully transparent.
...Was Mion always this terrible at lying?
...Her expression was different from usual, and yet... it looked strangely endearing.
``So, h-how was it?``
``...You two are like peas in a pod. Well, you’re twins, right?@k@vS20/01/440100072. Of course you’d look alike.``
``Um... that’s... that’s not what I... err...``
Mion had a kind of dazed expression, as if waiting for a specific answer.
...When she asked "how was it"—maybe she wasn’t asking about Shion’s appearance, but about the bento...?
changeReason: New correctly uses "how was it" matching the actual dialogue どうだった, while old inserted "impression" which was not in the text.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, of course! It was delicious.``
``...Eh... ah... really?``

``I never lie about food. If I say it was good, then anyone who tasted it would agree!@k@vS20/01/440100075. Consider it a universal recommendation.@k@vS20/01/440100076. Tell Shion it was truly excellent, will you?``
``Ah... o-okay! Tell Shion, right!@k@vS20/03/440300067. I’ll pass it along!@k@vS20/03/440300068. I’m sure she’ll be happy...!@k@vS20/03/440300069. Ahahahahaha...!``
Even though the pretense was that Shion—not Mion—had given me the bento...@kshe laughed with such genuine, heartfelt joy.
...So she could laugh like this too—a smile so pleasant that just watching it made you feel good.@kA wry remark rose to the tip of my tongue, but I swallowed it.
As Mion went to put the bento box in her bag, she noticed something rattling inside.
``...Kei-chan, there’s something in here. Huh?``
Geh... she opened it right here...!
changeReason: New includes "right here" translating この場で, adding the important context that Keiichi is upset she opened it in this specific location.

TextENGNew: The contents were a bit embarrassing, so I’d hoped she wouldn’t open it in front of everyone.
I panicked and covered it with my hands!
``Um... well, you see! My mom told me to put something in there as a thank-you!@k@vS20/01/440100078. It wasn’t my idea or anything!@k@vS20/01/440100079. Don’t get the wrong idea!!``
``...So pretty... Candy...``
Inside the sparkling-clean bento box was a handful of candies in pretty wrapping paper.
...Yesterday, when I was washing the bento box at the sink, Mom had "interrogated" me, and I’d been forced to confess who had brought it.
Then she insisted that you’re supposed to return the box with a thank-you gift inside.@k...I objected, saying it was embarrassing and not my style, but...
``...So... that’s what happened. ...I mean... ugh...``
I was so embarrassed I thought my face might catch fire.
...I would have preferred if Mion had just laughed it off like she usually did... but instead, she was gazing at the candy inside the box with an almost entranced expression.
``...Th-Thank you.``
``D-Don’t thank me. Thank my mom—she’s the one who put them in!@k@vS20/01/440100082. B-Besides, you don’t need to thank me at all, Mion!@k@vS20/01/440100083. Those are for Shion, got it?!``
changeReason: New correctly translates that Mion doesn't need to thank him (魅音がお礼を言う必要なんかない), while old incorrectly implies Keiichi should be thanking someone else.

TextENGNew: ``...Ah... right. You’re right...@k@vS20/03/440300075. ...Yeah.@k@vS20/03/440300076. I’ll make sure to tell Shion!@k@vS20/03/440300077. I’m sure she’ll be happy too.``
Mion closed the lid with a slightly disappointed look.@k...In the end, it seemed I’d said something a little cruel.
``Mii-chan seems kind of warm and fuzzy today. Did something good happen, I wonder?@k@vS20/02/440200056. I wonder!``
changeReason: New captures Rena's signature "I wonder! I wonder!" (かな！かな！) pattern and the specific mention of something good happening (いい事).

TextENGNew: ``...Maybe she’s caught a cold? The redness in her face is surely from a slight fever.``
changeReason: New correctly translates 風邪 as "cold" (not "fever") and 微熱 as "slight fever" (not "increased body temperature").

TextENGNew: ``...Hey, Rika. Why are you petting my head?``
``...Someday you’ll be able to catch that kind of fever too, Satoko.``
changeReason: New translation with "that kind of fever" better conveys Rika's double-meaning joke about love-sickness, adding nuance present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...*pat pat*
